title: Slugify with learned conventions
prompt:
Write a Python module that provides a function `slugify(title)` converting a title string into a URL slug.

Contract:
- Lowercase the input.
- Every maximal run of characters that are not ASCII letters or digits (spaces, underscores, punctuation, symbols) becomes a single hyphen.
- No leading or trailing hyphens in the result.
- Empty strings and non-string inputs are invalid.

Reply with the complete module in ONE fenced python code block.
judgeNotes: Objective task - scored by hidden gate tests; no judging needed.
tasteRules: Every function must have complete type annotations for all parameters and the return value.
Every public function must have a docstring stating its contract.
Invalid input must raise ValueError — never return None or a default — with the offending value in the message.
Every module must define __all__ listing its public API.
